steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank describes the type of evidence paleontologists must use to evaluate ideas about Pliocene environmental conditions. The sentence that follows defines what kind: "Only material objects—such as fossil remains—can show whether a particular idea is defensible." The blank must mean something like tangible or material.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Physical: means relating to material objects you can touch and examine—exactly what the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice B — Convenient: means easy or handy; has nothing to do with the kind of evidence described. ✗
  • Choice C — Creative: means imaginative; the passage is about grounding ideas in material fact, the opposite of creative speculation. ✗
  • Choice D — Inconclusive: means failing to settle the question; the passage says evidence must show whether an idea is defensible, so it must be meaningful, not inconclusive. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
